value.title: Nuclear Energy Safety: Data Reports 2026 - WifiTalents
value.content: # Nuclear Energy Safety Statistics. While our collective imagination often conjures up dramatic worst-case scenarios, the compelling reality of modern nuclear energy safety is one of the most statistically impressive success stories in human history, preventing an estimated 1.84 million air pollution-related deaths since the 1970s and boasting a death rate of just 0.07 per terawatt-hour, making it hundreds of times safer than fossil fuels. 3. 3The death rate for nuclear energy is 0.01 per TWh when excluding major historical accidents. 4. 4Over 440 commercial nuclear power reactors operate in 32 countries with high safety standards. The Convention on Nuclear Safety (CNS) requires peer reviews of national safety reports every 3 years. The death rate for nuclear energy is 0.01 per TWh when excluding major historical accidents. Replacing fossil fuels with nuclear energy has prevented an estimated 1.84 million air pollution-related deaths between 1971 and 2009. Over 440 commercial nuclear power reactors operate in 32 countries with high safety standards. epa.govLogo of world-nuclear.org. ### world-nuclear.org. world-nuclear.orgLogo of hps.org.

value.content: 100 for coal, 36 for oil, 24 for biofuel/biomass, 4 for natural gas, 1.4 for hydro, 0.44 for solar, 0.15 for wind and 0.04 for nuclear.

value.title: Nuclear has the fewest deaths per terawatt-hour. We've been afraid ...
value.content: It beats renewables and fossil fuels on most if not every relevant metric, truly a unicorn energy source. The only real “downside” is high
